Changzhou Huanghe Car Lamp Co., Ltd. presents a profile of an 1980s-era township enterprise that has functionally collapsed into a micro-shell. Founded in 1989 (originally 'Wujin Huanghe Car Lamp Factory'), its official classification as an 'Automobile Complete Vehicle Manufacturer' is absurd. After repairing the corrupted data fields, its actual business scope reveals a history of manufacturing low-end vehicle lamps, plastic granulation, and mechanical parts. Crucially, the addition of 'fabric textile processing' and 'non-medical daily masks' strongly indicates an opportunistic pivot during a crisis (likely the COVID-19 pandemic), attempting to survive by producing low-barrier textile goods. Ultimately, with a corrected workforce of exactly 1 insured employee in 2024 and no recorded paid-in capital, this entity has ceased all meaningful automotive or textile manufacturing and exists solely as a 'one-person' dormant shell.
